title: Solenopsis bivonae
taxonomy: Solenopsis bivonae is classified as a species within the Solenopsis genus of ants, which is part of the subfamily Myrmicinae. This genus is further categorized under the family Formicidae, commonly known as ants. The subfamily Myrmicinae is known to include various species of small to medium-sized ants. Solenopsis bivonae is native to the Mediterranean region and is considered an invasive species in some areas. It is a highly aggressive ant species known for its painful sting.

intro: Solenopsis bivonae, also known as the Canary Island fire ant plant, is a flowering plant native to the Canary Islands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing pain, swelling, and the risk of infection. The bioactive constituents of Solenopsis bivonae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tincture or infused oil, and is used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues.
